author: @scantrain5007
text:
The most inspiration for me is the shape of this rotor. The secret is as more lamells you have as less vibration is in the whole construction because the vibrations are the biggest problem by vertical wind turbines. 
Another aspect is the generator. I think it might be a good solution to use old electric motors (from old drill, motor from law miller etc) as generator and mount them direct on the shaft of rotor. Means: No gear or belt drive needed. Less parts means less potential damages / errors in the system.

author: @ThisRandomUsername
text: As others have said, the pancake coil is exactly the wrong arrangement for rotating magnets around the assembly. Your magnets need to cut across the wire perpendicularly. What I'd do is exactly what your second arrangement did. You could have one coil for every 2 magnets and wire them in series or parallel for high voltage or high current.

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text:
Total flux change in the pancake coil is 0. You did the same with the turntable coil a while ago with little result as well. Any significant result is not to be expected. The turntable motor had a meander pattern inside constructed of the iron plating which made it a working concept. That's the whole reason why such a motor works with the coil arranged in such a way.  
If you stick an iron cross on one side of the pancake coil ( 2 pcs of transformer steel would do great ( maybe 4 if you can't span from magnet to magnet )) and another one on the other side rotated 45 degrees it will generate. It would make an awesome new video :-) and it's easy to do.

author: @ryanlebeck259
text:
Have you considered placing magnets along the fan and having the fan pass across coils under it? I have a friend that is using a V configuration on a fan like that and he is getting interesting results from passing the fan along coils mounted on the base board.

The magnets don't create a great deal of drag and the fan is VERY well balanced and can tolerate the magnets with ease.

I find your work with magnetic assist might also be applied here on the side of the board opposite the magnets with the coil in between.

The magnets will create flux and perhaps some drag on the fan, though the extra flux might balance out the generation within the coil as the magnetic fields interact with the coil as a "mid way point" between the two.

Give it a think will you, and another lesson learned 😁. 

Keep on it; your videos are informative and you don't conceal your small failures and gains.

Thank you for sharing.

author: @MrTubeuser12
text: reluctance motors are making a bit of a resurgence , Tesla uses them in their latest cars, there are even some EV conversion specialists who convert induction motors into reluctance motors by replacing the rotor and using a different controller, but that makes it a synchronous reluctance motor, not switched reluctance, still, this is definitely something worth  getting involved in. great video as always.

author: @ShilohSedlak
text: Magnets crossing wires makes voltage.. Pancake coils you are running the Magnets lengthwise with the wires.. This is the reason why you got no voltage to speak of.

author: @totherarf
text:
As to your pancake coil .... if you don't fail sometimes you are not trying enough!
As others have pointed out you need to have a coil breaking the lines of magnetic flux to generate (eather force or power). The reason they use pancake coils in generators is that it allows low maintainance coupled with fairly easily achieved close tolerances .... don't forget they are usually several coils potted together to form a doughnut not just one coil.

Your pancake coil could end up generating low power but high voltage .... check out Current Transformers to see why ;0)

author: @NickGrumpy
text: I think the problem with the pancake coil is you had the magnets travailing adjacent to the wire and not crossing its path. I think you are on the right track with the VR system

author: @chris993361
text: With the pancake coil the magnetic field is moving parallel to the wires but the magnetic field has to cut perpendicular to the wires to generate voltage. If you did something like not have them share a center axis but move the pancake coil up to where the magnets cross its center point then they would be passing perpendicular to the wires in two places and give you much higher voltage. Fun to watch you experiment as always.

author: @ezequieladrianminniti6252
text: I've tried several times to make generators, but no matter what, the most efficient yet cheapest by far you are gonna get is a phantom drone brushless motor, attached to your turbine. the trick is that those motors need thigh rpm, so diy the right gear ratio to convert the high torque of your wind turbine to run the drone motor very fast. and you have super efficient 3 phase ac!
